violations.text: The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Space observed between the 2 doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2025

Employees observed washing their hands at the prep sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. One employee observed washing gloved hands in the handsink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025
Employee observed putting on gloves without washing her hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (cheese when slicing) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Voluntary disposal during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Small refrigerator at the coffee station was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Milk observed with a temperature of 45 degrees F. Prep unit observed with an ambient temperature of 48 degrees F. Foods in this unit observed with temperatures 45 - 46 degrees F. Products were moved into this unit this morning. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. TCS products were relocated to a properly functioning refrigeration units. No TCS food products are to be stored in these units until they are able to maintain a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. No employee certified in food safety management was on-site at the time of inspection. In addition, the RI Certified Food Safety Manager license has expired for this establishment. Employee(s) must register in, and successfully complete, an approved course in food safety management. One full-time employee must register with RIDOH as Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

2-door produce reach-in observed with an ambient temperature of 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Raw chicken was observed with a temperature of 36 degrees F and was moved to a properly functioning unit. No TCS foods are to be stored in this unit until it is serviced and maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

Deli meats, cheeses, and prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by small flies observed at the front counter handsink. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control is to be contacted to evaluate and treat as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 2-door produce reach-in were observed in poor repair, split away from the door. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
